title: [Bug] GetOrderBook returns internal pointer — breaks thread safety
body:
## Problem

`GetOrderBook()` returns a direct pointer to the internal `*orderbook.OrderBook`. Callers can read and mutate the order book without holding the engine's mutex, creating data races.

```go
book := e.GetOrderBook("BTC")
// This reads book.Bids without any lock, while another goroutine
// may be modifying it via SubmitOrder
for _, b := range book.Bids {
    fmt.Println(b.Price)
}
```

This is a race condition that can cause panics, corrupted reads, or index-out-of-range errors under concurrent access.

## Affected Files
- `pkg/engine/engine.go` — `GetOrderBook()` method

## Proposed Fix
Option A: Return a deep copy (snapshot) of the order book.
```go
func (e *Engine) GetOrderBook(symbol string) *orderbook.OrderBook {
    e.mu.Lock()
    defer e.mu.Unlock()
    book := e.books[symbol]
    if book == nil {
        return nil
    }
    return book.Snapshot() // deep copy
}
```

Option B: Return a read-only view struct with only the data needed (best bid, best ask, depth, price levels).

## Priority
High — concurrency bug.
